insight: When holding Colossus for soak absorption versus rebuying Wall, the temporary soak gain on that turn (8 soak) differs from long-term efficiency; rebuying Wall the next turn costs 3 soak, so net long-run gain is only 5 soak despite the 8 soak temporary value.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of absorption mechanics and soak efficiency calculations.

insight: Savior is difficult to model for inflation calculations because its value depends heavily on game-specific variables: number of drones bought, which turns they're bought, number of Plasmafiers made, which turns Plasmafiers are clicked, and how many are used for soak. Each game requires separate analysis rather than a universal metric.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of whether Savior can be compared to other slow strategies like Zemora using inflation metrics.

insight: In a Drone-Drone-Conduit/Drone-Drone-Blastforge opening, avoiding giving opponent early absorb by buying Tantalum Ray or Kinetic (for later Conduit sniping) is preferable to certain alternatives.
author: jamberine
context: Opening analysis regarding early unit choices to avoid opponent advantage.

insight: Redeemer is viable in base set P2 response to P1 asymmetric access. P1 line DD DD DDB SDC wins on turn 5 against P2's TTD by using Redeemer effectively, and also beats P2's TTE with WGE line.
author: mtanzer, silentslayers
context: Testing Redeemer viability in base set mirror with asymmetric access.

insight: Against Venge Cannon all-in strategies, mono green with good soak and good absorbers (like Energy Matrix) provides sustainable defensive options. Quick expensive damage (like Tesla) forces Venge player to build Venges rather than continue droning, which may or may not be advantageous.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion of counterplay to Venge Cannon all-in rush strategies.

insight: Going breach-proof forfeits the right to an absorber; if the set has a strong absorber (e.g., Energy Matrix), this loss is significant. Breach-proof becomes stronger with Freeze (especially Nivo), breach-proof drones, and breach-proof attackers (especially Cluster Bolt).
author: velizar_
context: Analysis of breach-proof strategy tradeoffs and synergies.

insight: Zemora Voidbringer can survive against Chrono + Wincer if bought early enough that only one Wincer shot lands before Zemora is online, combined with good soak like Doomed Wall and Doomed Drone. After surviving two shots, Zemora player likely wins.
author: megasupp
context: Post-game analysis of asymmetric Zemora versus Chrono Wincer matchup.

insight: In sets with good absorbers like Omega Splitter (with blue sink), buying extra economy is often correct. However, holding clickable absorbers instead of clicking them provides more value than clicking them; comparing Omega Splitter held versus clicked shows holding generates more total defense benefit via better defensive unit purchases.
author: jamberine
context: Discussion of absorber mechanics and economy tradeoffs in defensive positioning.

insight: High-quality soak units (like Aegis) come in large chunks requiring significant opponent attack (e.g., 7+ attack) before providing value, making it difficult to efficiently use multiple copies. This limits the economy-boosting effect of strong soak compared to strong absorbers.
author: jamberine
context: Analysis of why strong soak doesn't encourage high economy as much as strong absorbers do.

insight: Master Bot has difficulty holding Drones late-game when Attackers are already built, often buying Drones when Attackers or other units would be stronger. In specific endgame positions, Drone purchases can still be optimal over Tarsier (e.g., Splitter + Husk > Tarsier + Husk), but Master Bot applies this heuristic incorrectly.
author: masn6811, velizar_
context: Analysis of Master Bot endgame errors in unit purchasing priorities.

insight: Felid is strong even as a first attacker when Animus is available; the Bombarder rule (buy some permanent attack before Bombarder) suggests buying all available Felid supply when paired with Animus.
author: velizar_
context: Discussion of Felid's strength relative to other early attackers.

insight: Evis requires Red mana to click and this cost is easy to forget during gameplay; proper costing analysis must account for the click cost in addition to purchase cost.
author: velizar_
context: Brief note on Evis mechanics to avoid missed opportunities.

insight: Old Grenade Mech with 0 click cost would enable spam-clicking similar to Drake spam, but required building up economy to sustain Blastforge cycling. The 2-gold cost difference (Grenade Mech vs Drake) plus 1 extra damage per click (3 vs 2) made it significantly stronger than Drake spam strategies.
author: masn6811
context: Historical discussion of Grenade Mech balance changes and why 0 click cost was overpowered.

insight: Perforator can be used efficiently as an early attacker paired with economy like Animus; the ability to sell Red later allows flexibility to stop spending Red and pivot to defense or other priorities.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion of Perforator's versatility as both attacker and economic outlet.

insight: Infusion Grid is a soft counter to Wincer strategies; buying at least one Plexo Cell (with Conduit for Green) provides a minimum defense floor that limits Wincer's exploitability, even though multiple Plexos can be exploited more heavily.
author: velizar_
context: Analysis of defensive options against Wincer rush strategies.

insight: In Black Lab, the probability of flipping a color you don't have (like White with only Blue unlocked) is very low (~1 in 30), making it extremely expensive to convert shards into lab tickets for color-specific farming unless you specifically want Infusions.
author: jamberine
context: Discussion of Black Lab shard efficiency and color unlocking probabilities.
